position_summary: Claude's v2 prose materially fixed the main round-1 correctness blockers: triage is now scoped correctly, `ac_known` is the Open Targets overall association score, the disease hop is mostly kept as association evidence, and the self-audit/no-API claims are bounded. I would not call sections 1-3 fully converged yet: the draft is close, but a few residual shorthand phrases and newly introduced control/guardrail claims still create reviewer attack surface.

findings.title: Residual `disease-supported` shorthand still blurs association nomination in the funnel summary
findings.evidence: Most disease-hop language is now correct, especially `docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:51-52`, `:73-77`, `02_background.md:55-60`, and `03_methods.md:98-105`. But `docs/manuscript/sections/03_methods.md:83-85` still says the referee keeps `chain-supported survivors` and reports `43 disease-supported -> 30 clean full-chain nominations`.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer skimming the Methods funnel may quote `disease-supported` as evidence that the manuscript still treats the disease hop as support rather than GWAS/enrichment nomination. This is much narrower than the round-1 problem, but it is still a vulnerable compressed phrase in a high-density Methods paragraph.
findings.suggested_action: Replace `43 disease-supported` with `43 disease-hop association-positive` or `43 disease-enrichment-positive`, and use `chain-held` / `receipt-complete` instead of `chain-supported` in that order-of-operations summary.

findings.title: The negative-control panel is now named, but the label-shuffle expectation is statistically unclear
findings.evidence: `docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:68-70` says label-shuffled disease assignments have a `refutation rate should approach chance`. The outline repeats `refutation rate rises to ~chance` at `docs/manuscript/OUTLINE.md:157-159` and `:294-296`, but sections 1-3 do not define the null rate, the denominator, or why `chance` is the expected direction.
findings.concern_path: A methods reviewer can ask what chance means in a multi-disease enrichment table with sparse positives. Under many label-shuffle designs, the expected result is not intuitively `chance refutation`; it may be disease-prevalence-driven support, near-universal refutation, or a permutation null distribution. The control is valuable, but the current one-clause claim sounds hand-wavy.
findings.suggested_action: Change the Introduction to a non-quantified preview such as `label-shuffled disease assignments, which estimate the null disease-hop pass rate`, and leave the exact expected rate/null distribution to Section 4.

findings.title: Section metadata still says `DRAFT v1` after a v2 revision
findings.evidence: The section headers still read `DRAFT v1` in `docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:3`, `02_background.md:3`, and `03_methods.md:3`, even though this round is explicitly prose v2 after accepted round-1 edits.
findings.concern_path: This is not a scientific flaw, but it creates provenance noise. A collaborator or reviewer can reasonably wonder whether they are reading the pre-debate draft or the revised manuscript prose.
findings.suggested_action: Update the headers to `DRAFT v2` or remove the version label from the manuscript-facing files and keep versioning in the debate artifacts.

findings.title: No-API guardrails are better, but `confined network` overstates what the prose proves
findings.evidence: `docs/manuscript/sections/03_methods.md:131-139` says the driver auto-approves network-access cards and that network access is `confined to the named data and literature endpoints a task requires`. Repo context supports network-domain approvals and cache guards, but the section does not say whether confinement is enforced by a workbench allowlist, prompt discipline, driver matching, or merely by task design.
findings.concern_path: A security-minded reviewer may read `auto-approves network-access cards` plus `confined` as claiming a technical allowlist. If the actual guarantee is auditability plus endpoint-specific approval cards plus replay guard for the full sweep, the current wording is slightly stronger than the evidence in the prose.
findings.suggested_action: Say `network approvals were granted only for the named endpoints used by the task` or explicitly name the enforcement mechanism if there is a true allowlist. Keep the no-API driver claim sharp; just avoid implying a stronger sandbox policy than was demonstrated.

escalated_findings.rationale: Mostly addressed, but not fully closed: one Methods funnel sentence still uses `disease-supported` / `chain-supported` shorthand where the disease hop should remain association-positive or enrichment-positive.
Partially addressed: the control classes are now named, but the new label-shuffle `chance` claim is too undefined for a Methods/Demonstration paper.

dropped_findings.rationale: Resolved. §2.1 explicitly says the method closes the triage loop, not the experimental-follow-up loop; §1 ends with the bench-time triage question.
Resolved enough for sections 1-3. The opening now uses `testable connections` / `candidate connection`, not unbounded discovery language.
Resolved. §3.1 and §3.2 correctly describe `ac_known` as the Open Targets overall target-disease association score and separate it from T3 genetic disease labels.
Resolved. §3.3 concedes the funnel pre-gating and points broad four-hop discrimination to the out-of-funnel ledger without losing the confident-NO claim.

Resolved for manuscript prose. `supported` is now explicitly a bounded verdict label, and §3.4 scopes calibrated-language enforcement to manuscript-facing output while acknowledging the legacy `validated` log.
Resolved. The self-audit claim is now role/model/checkpoint independence within one model family, with cross-family replication external.
Substantively addressed. The no-API driver now has operator-session, sandbox, fixed-workspace, logged-approval, and replay-guard language; only the narrower F-016 wording issue remains.
Resolved. The grant identifier is absent from sections 1-3; the prose keeps `proposal submitted for funding consideration`.
Resolved. §2.2 now explains the held substrate as bounded adjudication surface, not a universal truth oracle.
Resolved. §3.2 now says the gene universe is disease-answer-free, not evidence-free.
Resolved for Methods. §3.2 now states the objective and weights were fixed before the sweep, separates ranking from verdict, and points sensitivity to Section 4.
Resolved. The NAB2 novelty criterion is now operationalized as low direct-literature plus low curated association, not strict A-C absence.

recommended_next_question: Round 3 should verify a small prose cleanup: replace the remaining `disease-supported` shorthand, make the label-shuffle control describe a null distribution rather than undefined `chance`, update stale draft metadata, and clarify whether network confinement is an enforced allowlist or logged endpoint-specific approval.
